CURRICULUM VITAE.


Name:			Pierre COLAS

Date of birth:		January 3 , 1966

Place of birth:		Chatou, France

Nationality:		French

Address:		Department of Molecular Biology
			Massachusetts General Hospital
			BOSTON,  MA 02114  USA

Tel:			(1) 617 726 5956
Fax:			(1) 617 726 6893
e.mail:			colas@frodo.mgh.harvard.edu


EDUCATION:
1982
Baccalauréat série C obtained in Caen, Normandy.

1982/83
Preparation to a "grande école de commerce" lycée St. Just, Lyon.

1983/85
Deug B (Undergraduate Degree in Biology) Université de Lyon.

1985/86
Licence of Cellular Biology and Physiology, option Genetics, Université de Lyon. (Certificates in Genetics, Molecular Biology, Immunology, Metabolic Biochemistry and Enzymology, General Physiology).

1986/87
Master of Science (Genetics). Université de Lyon (Certificates in Molecular and Cellular Genetics, Genetics of populations, Cellular Interactions and Differentiation, Mathematics applied to Biology). Report concerning the molecular evolution of the genes encoding the apocytochrome B.

1987/88
DEA of Differenciation, Genetics and Immunology. Université de Lyon.

Sept.1987 to April 1988 : with Lê-Thi-Bich-Thuy at the Laboratory of Molecular and Cellular Biology of the Ecole Normale Supérieure de Lyon. Prepared a bibliographic report concerning the IL-2 receptors expressed on the human T cells.

April1988 to Aug. 1988 : Research with Pr. G. Brun at the same laboratory. My work was to study the mitochondrial DNA of quail neuroretina cells transformed by the Rous Sarcoma Virus and to compare it with the mitochondrial DNA of normal cells. This experimental work has been the subject of a report for my DEA.

1988/91
PhD Research with Dr. P. Guerrier at the same laboratory. General topic of interest : Molecular Biology of meiosis reinitiation in marine invertebrate oocytes. The main focus on my thesis work was to try to understand the molecular mechanisms of regulation of the M-phase factor (MPF) that governs the entry into the M-phase.

1992/93
General Economics at the Institute for Economics and Management of Utrecht (The Netherlands), European Program. Certificates in microeconomics and macroeconomics.



HONORS AND AWARDS:

1985 Mention Assez Bien (Honors) for the DEUG.

1987 Mention Assez Bien (Honors) for the Master of Science.

1988 Ranked in the top 15% for the DEA. Ranked 2 out of 25 for the governmental doctoral grant.

1989 Fellowship from the fondation Jacques Cartier for the trip to Rimouski, Canada .

1990 EMBO short term fellowship for a program of research of 3 months in Utrecht, The Netherlands.

1991 Mention Très Honorable with the congratulations from the Jury for the PhD.

1992 Fellowship from the Association de la Recherche contre le Cancer (ARC) for a one year post-doctoral period in Utrecht, The Netherlands.

1993 Fellowship from the Ligue Nationale Française contre le Cancer for a one year post-doctoral period in Utrecht, The Netherlands.

1994 EMBO long term fellowship for a one year post-doctoral period in Boston, USA. 1995 EMBO long term fellowship renewed for another year in Boston, USA.

1996 Ranked in the top 10% at the CNRS INSERM job applications.



RESEARCH:
May1989 to Aug.1989, research with Pr. F. Dubé and Pr. L. Dufresne at the Département d'Océanographie de l'Université du Québec à Rimouski, CANADA. Work on the cyclins of the blue mussel Mytilus edulis. Isolation of tubulin and micro-tubule-associated proteins of the sea urchin Strongylocentrotus droebachiensis .

Aug.1990 to Nov.1990, research with Dr. A. van Loon at the Department of Experimental Zoology directed by Pr. J. van den Biggelaar at the university of Utrecht, The Netherlands. Construction of two cDNA libraries ((lambda)gt11) in Patella vulgata and immunoscreenings. Directed mutagenesis of the cyclin cDNAs of Patella vulgata.

March1992 to Oct. 1993, Post-doctoral period at the Department of Experimental Zoology directed by Pr. J. van den Biggelaar at the university of Utrecht, The Netherlands. Work on the regulation of cyclin proteolysis and on the role of the suc1 gene product in cell cycle regulation in Patella vulgata oocytes and embryos.

Nov. 1993 -Post-doctoral period in Roger Brent's lab. at the Department of Molecular Biology of the Massachusetts General Hospital / Department of Genetics of Harvard Medical School, Boston, MA. USA. 2 research projects :
1- Caracterisation of peptide aptamers isolated by the two-hybrid system which recognize and inhibit human cyclin-dependent kinase 2. Development of a derived intracellular nanotechnology.
2- Study of human cDNAs isolated in yeast by virtue of their ability to positively regulate cell cycle.



PUBLICATIONS:
Guerrier P., Colas P. and Néant I. (1990). Meiosis reinitiation as a model system for the study of cell division and cell differentiation. Int. J. Dev. Biol. 34: 93-109.

Guerrier P., Néant I., Colas P., Dufresne L., Saint-Pierre J. and Dubé F. (1990). Protein synthesis and protein phosphorylation as regulators of MPF activity. In "Mechanism of fertilization, Plants to Humans", B. Dale. Ed. Springer-Verlag, Berlin.

van Loon A.*, Colas P*., Goedemans H., Néant I., and Guerrier P. (1991). The role of cyclins in the maturation of the oocyte of Patella vulgata . EMBO J. 10: 3343-3349. * Equal contribution of the two first authors.

Colas P. (1991). Contrôle moléculaire de la division cellulaire : Rôle des cyclines lors de l'entrée et du maintien des ovocytes de Patella vulgata en phase M. Thèse de doctorat, Université LYON I.

Lorca T., Devault A., Colas P., van Loon A., Fesquet,D., Lazaro J.B. and Dorée M. (1992). Cyclin A-Cys41 does not undergo cell cycle-dependent degradation in Xenopus extracts. FEBS Lett. 306: 90-93.

Colas P., Launay C., van Loon A. and Guerrier P. (1993). Protein synthesis controls cyclin stability in metaphase I-arrested oocytes of Patella vulgata . Exp. Cell Res. 208 : 518-521.

Colas P., Serras F. and van Loon A. (1993). Deregulated expression of suc1 affects the cell cycle clock in Patella vulgata embryos. Int. J. Dev. Biol. 37: 589-594.

Abdelmajid H., Guerrier P., Colas P., Durocher Y., Gobet I., Krantic S., Leclerc-David C., Moreau M., Néant I., Rivailler P., and Tomkowiak M. (1993) Role of calcium during release of mollusc oocytes from their blocks in meiotic prophase and metaphase. Biol. Cell 78: 137-143.

Colas P. and Guerrier P. (1995). "The oocyte metaphase arrest". In "Progress in Cell Cycle Research" L.Meijer &H.Y. Lim Tung Eds. Plenum Press, New York, USA, pp 299-308.

*Colas P., *Cohen B., Jessen T., Grishina I., McCoy J. and Brent R. (1996). Genetic selection of peptide aptamers that recognize and inhibit cyclin- dependent kinase 2. Nature 380: 548-550. *Equal contribution of the two first authors.


COMMUNICATIONS:

International meeting on signal transduction and gene activation in development, Streamboat Springs, USA. 1990. A. van Loon, H. Goedemans, I. Daemen, A. van de Kamp, P. Colas, P. Guerrier & J. van den Biggelaar. "Gene activation during early development of the marine mollusc Patella vulgata ".

Fourth International Franco-Czechoslovak Meeting, "Through the oocyte to the embryo" Prague. 1990. " Protein phosphorylation and the cell cycle : effects of 6-DMAP". I. Néant, L. Dufresne, P. Colas, F. Dubé & P. Guerrier.

J.Monod conference, " The cell cycle", Roscoff, France, 1990. " Cyclins and the control of the metaphase arrest in invertebrates oocytes." P.Colas, A. van Loon, F. Dubé & P. Guerrier

International meeting organized by the Société de Biologie Cellulaire de France. "Contrôle de la Division Cellulaire", Lyon,France, 1992. "Regulation of cyclin proteolysis in Patella vulgata oocytes". Invited speaker.

Jacques Monod Conference. "Eukaryotic Cell Cycle Controls", Aussois, France, 1992. "Regulation of cyclin proteolysis in Patella vulgata oocytes". Selected speaker.

Washington International Spring Symposium. "The cell cycle 1993: regulators, targets and clinical applications." Washington D.C., USA, 1993. "Deregulation of suc1 expression affects the cell cycle clock." Selected speaker.

Jacques Monod Conference. "The Cell Division Cycle", Aussois, France, 1995. Selected poster.


6th International Workshop on the Identification of Transcribed Sequences, Edinburgh, Scotland, 1996. "New nays of charting and exploring regulatory networks." selected speaker.



TEACHING/SUPERVISING:

1991/92 :
One-year supervision of Catherine Launay, a DEA student working on the regulation of cyclin proteolysis in the group of Dr. P. Guerrier, Lyon, France.

Dec 1994 :
Participant in a Mini-symposium about the two hybrid technology, held at Harvard Medical School, Boston.

Summer 1996:
Supervision of Thais Perkins, summer student in the group of Dr. Roger Brent, Boston.
